name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale (GAD-7)
disclaimerBefore: This questionnaire is for educational purposes only and is NOT a clinical diagnosis. Results are for personal reflection. If you have concerns or severe symptoms, please consult a healthcare professional. Your answers are stored only in your browser.
disclaimerAfter: This test is NOT a clinical diagnosis and does NOT replace evaluation by a professional. If you have severe or persistent symptoms, please consult your doctor or psychologist.
instructions: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

landing.description: The GAD-7 is a clinically validated questionnaire that assesses the frequency and intensity of generalized anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks. It is one of the most widely used screening tools in primary care worldwide.
landing.whatItMeasures: It measures 7 key symptoms of generalized anxiety disorder: nervousness, inability to control worry, excessive worrying, difficulty relaxing, restlessness, irritability, and anticipatory fear.
landing.whoIsItFor: Adults (18+) experiencing anxiety symptoms, or anyone who wants to assess their emotional wellbeing. Also useful for sharing results with a mental health professional.
howItWorks: Answer 7 questions about the past 2 weeks
Get your score with a clear clinical interpretation
Share the result with a professional if needed

faq.a: No. The GAD-7 is a screening tool, not a diagnosis. Only a qualified mental health professional can diagnose an anxiety disorder. This test helps you identify whether your symptoms warrant professional attention.
Yes. All answers are processed in your browser and never sent to any server. We do not store any personal data.

faq.q: What should I do if I score high?
faq.a: We recommend consulting your primary care doctor or a psychologist. You can bring your result printed or on-screen to your appointment.

faq.q: Can I share my results with my therapist?
faq.a: Yes. When you finish, you will be able to share a link with your result. Many professionals use the GAD-7 in clinical practice and will recognize the score.
Between 2 and 3 minutes. There are only 7 questions with 4 response options each.

questions.text: How often have you been feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge?
How often have you been unable to stop or control worrying?
How often have you been worrying too much about different things?
How often have you had trouble relaxing?
How often have you been so restless that it was hard to sit still?
How often have you become easily annoyed or irritable?
How often have you felt afraid as if something awful might happen?

minimal.title: Minimal Anxiety
minimal.body: Your anxiety symptoms are within normal ranges. It is natural to experience some worry in daily life.
minimal.recommendation: Continue maintaining good wellness habits: regular sleep, exercise, and activities you enjoy.

mild.title: Mild Anxiety
mild.body: You are experiencing mild anxiety symptoms. While they are not significantly interfering with your daily life, you could benefit from stress management techniques.
mild.recommendation: Try relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, meditation, or regular exercise. If symptoms persist, consider speaking with a professional.

moderate.title: Moderate Anxiety
moderate.body: Your anxiety symptoms are moderate and may be affecting your quality of life in some areas (work, relationships, sleep). Seeking professional support is recommended.
moderate.recommendation: It is RECOMMENDED that you consult a psychologist or doctor to assess your symptoms and receive appropriate treatment.

severe.title: Severe Anxiety
severe.body: Your anxiety symptoms are significant and are likely affecting your wellbeing and daily functioning. Seeking professional help is important.
severe.recommendation: We encourage you to contact a mental health professional as soon as possible. You can call the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline if you need immediate support.

crisis.title: You need to talk to someone now
crisis.body: Some of your answers suggest you may be going through a very difficult time. You do not have to face this alone. There are people available right now to listen to you.
